page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is an entry-level virtual private server belonging to the High Performance family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include a GPU. The CPU features 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and AES cryptography.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the server is suited for low-intensity tasks. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves an average score of 1421.08, placing it in the middle 50% tier. However, due to its single-core shared CPU allocation, multi-core performance is limited, scoring 1421.08, which falls into the bottom 10% of benchmarked systems. Memory bandwidth is also modest, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 107.08 GB/sec and large block uncached read and write speeds at 9.16 GB/sec and 7.89 GB/sec respectively.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for hosting basic services without dedicated hardware overhead. The inclusion of 25 GB of NVMe SSD storage provides fast local storage access relative to the compute constraints. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 1.37 GB/sec, basic Redis caching supporting 1.58 million SET operations per second, microservices, development environments, and small-scale testing.
description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is an entry-level virtual server in the High Performance family, configured with a single shared Intel Xeon vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance in the average tier with a stress-ng score of 1421.08, while its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles due to the shared single-core design. The server delivers 1.37 GB/sec of static web serving throughput and handles 1.58 million Redis SET operations per second. This instance provides a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, budget-conscious deployments. It is best suited for basic web serving, microservices, small Redis caches, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 25 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight web serving and testing.

tagline: A budget-friendly, single-core virtual server with NVMe SSD storage for lightweight web serving, microservices, and development environments.
